title: eDNA Extraction Protocol from Water Samples Using GFF Filters and Bento Lab
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"elboe","text":"A protocol aiming at DNA exctraction from filters using spin columns.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"7rl4p","text":"Bento Lab (centrifuge, heat block, PCR thermocycler, blue light illuminator)\nMicrocentrifuge tubes (1.5 mL, sterile)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"agt0i","text":"Pipettes (P200, P1000) with sterile filtered tips","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4hlln","text":"Vortex mixer (optional but recommended)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9jad1","text":"Ethanol 96–100% (molecular biologygrade)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"795iu","text":"Qiagen DNeasy Blood and Tissue Kit\nGFF filters in 5 mL tubes with 1 mL ATL buffer (Qiagen)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bf54c","text":"Proteinase K (included in DNeasy Blood and Tissue Kits)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ffa7b","text":"Nuclease-free water","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fhhva","text":"PCR tubes and reagents ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4nash","text":"Primers 10 µM","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2p8oi","text":"Master mix Qiagen Multiplex PCR Plus Kit","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Add 20 µL of Proteinase K to each 5 mL tube containing the filter and ATL buffer.
Vortex briefly or manually shake to mix.
Incubate the tubes in the Bento Lab heat block at 56 °C for 1–3 hours, or overnight for better yield. Ensurefilters are fully submerged.
After the incubation, transfer 400 µL of the lysate (avoiding large filterfragments) into a new 1.5 mL tube.
Add 400 µL volume (1:1) of AL buffer to the lysate and mix thoroughly.
Add 400 µL volume of 96–100% ethanol and mix again (vortex or pipette up and down).
Apply 600 µL of the mixture to a Qiagen spin column in steps; centrifuge at 6,000–8,000 rpm for 1 min in Bento Lab centrifuge.
Discard flow-through each time and repeat until all of the lysate from the 1,5 mL tubeis centrifuged through the column.
Perform the wash steps according to the original protocol
Place spin column in a new 1.5 mL Eppendorf tube.
Add 80 µL of Buffer AE or nuclease-free water.
Incubate at room temperature for 1 min, centrifuge 1 min to elute DNA.
An Inhibitor removal can be performed on each sample or the samples can be stored at -20 °C until further analysis.
creator: Vid Švara
